issue_title: empty list in one filter causes the whole action to fail
issue_body:
Already discussed this internally with @untergeek , opening it up for a public discussion.

Creating an action to add and remove indices to an alias (e.g. last 7 days), is an atomic operation and fails if one of the filers receives an empty list. I.e. if this action is run, while the cluster only has indices for the last 5 days, the `remove` filter would fail, causing the whole action to fail.  

Also reported via: https://discuss.elastic.co/t/curator-alias-add-not-working-if-remove-list-is-empty/73549

Example config:
```
actions:
        last-7-days: 
                action: alias 
                description: "Add / remove indexes from last-7-days alias"
                options: 
                        name: last-7-days 
                        ignore_empty_list: True 
                add: 
                        filters: 
                                - filtertype: pattern 
                                  kind: prefix 
                                  value: logstash- 
                                - filtertype: age 
                                  direction: younger 
                                  source: name 
                                  timestring: '%Y.%m.%d' 
                                  unit_count: 7 
                                  unit: days 
                remove: 
                        filters: 
                                - filtertype: pattern 
                                  kind: prefix 
                                  value: losgstash- 
                                - filtertype: age 
                                  direction: older 
                                  source: name 
                                  timestring: '%Y.%m.%d' 
                                  unit_count: 7 
                                  unit: days
``` 

On one side I feel this behavior is correct, the action instructs curator to do these two steps and since one is failing, the whole actions fails. Just continuing with one filter failed, could lead to unexpected behavior. E.g. if filters or the whole action depend on each other. 
But in case of the alias I can understand that user exception is to just add the indices and skip the remove part until there are indices in the cluster that can be removed from the index. 

Just continuing with a failed filter doesn't sound like a good approach either, even in case of aliases.
Imagine relaying on a query that uses a last 7 days alias, if the remove "silently" fails, it could lead to this alias returning results for the last 3 weeks instead, resulting in wrong data.

Purposing to make this a configurable option for the user (to be "ok" with failing a specific or any part of the action).

issue_title: Snapshot name with timestamp always uses UTC
issue_body:
I am using curator to create a daily snapshot of some indices and use the following action definiton for this:


```javascript
actions:
 1:
    action: snapshot
    description: Snapshot everything beginning with analytics- 
    options:
      repository: backup_server
      name: 'analytics-%Y%m%d%H%M%S'
      ignore_unavailable: False
      include_global_state: True
      partial: False
      wait_for_completion: True
      skip_repo_fs_check: False
      timeout_override:
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    filters:
    - filtertype: pattern
      kind: prefix
      value: analytics-
      exclude:
```

When I test this a snapshot is created, however the timestring used for the snapshot name is in UTC, while the server timezone is UTC+2. So for example when I run curator at 13:00 local time, the snapshot will be created with a timestring of "110000", not adjusted for the servers timezone.

I couldn't find any setting to change this behavior, but maybe I am missing something here. From looking at the [code](https://github.com/elastic/curator/blob/master/curator/utils.py#L1013):
```
 rendered += str(datetime.utcnow().strftime('%{0}'.format(curr)))
```
I get the impression that UTC is simply assumed, though I am not a python guy and won't pretend that I understood everything that is happening.

I think it would be useful to change this to adjust for the timezone configured on the server, or introduce an optional parameter to pass in a timezone to use. 
It would probably be good to also be able to add timezone information to the snapshot name in this case to avoid confusion.

issue_title: Jupyter terminal using different sys.path from Jupyter notebook (running on same server, same conda env)
issue_body: I was having a common package import issue that was mostly resolved by several similar issues (https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/2359, https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/397, https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/2898), but now I have a new issue that I don't think has been mentioned here. I am using conda on macOS 10.12. If activate a conda env with `source activate $envname`, then run `python` from my local terminal, I can easily import the packages that I have installed in my envname.yml file. From this conda env I can start a `jupyter notebook` and run a _Jupyter_ terminal. In the Jupyter terminal, `sys.executable` returns the same result as in my local terminal, and I can import all the same packages. HOWEVER, if I start a new Jupyter notebook from the same launcher, running side-by-side the functioning Jupyter terminal on the same server, `sys.executable` returns a DIFFERENT path (one that I thought I had deleted). Of course, this means I cannot import the package I hope to use in the notebook. Not only that, but the list of `sys.path` locations in the Jupyter terminal versus the Jupyter notebook are completely non-overlapping---see screenshots below. It seems from the above screenshots that the Jupyter terminal is choosing to run python from a different conda env than the Jupyter notebook. To solve this problem I removed the wrong env that the notebook was using as sys.path with `conda env remove`. Now, there are no kernel errors when I start the Jupyter launcher or a Jupyter terminal, but starting a notebook returns a kernel error, as if it is expecting to work only with the path listed in the removed conda env.

issue_title: Display a file description in the notebook file browser for a better overview in larger projects
issue_body:
In larger projects it can get a bit annoying to find one particular file with "those particular lines I really need to see right now". So, in order to find it more quickly, how about display the content of the first cell if it's a markdown cell in the overview?

It could look something like this:

![screenshot from 2017-01-31 18-16-53](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1429426/22476108/76dba9b6-e7e1-11e6-9637-ca8a85b96c8a.png)

----

**Further ideas:**

- This function could be turned on and off with a simple **enable/disable checkbox**
- Another option to implement this could be a **tooltip** that shows only if the mouse hovers over the filename
- Instead of *always* display the first markdown cell, there could be a dedicated "**file description cell**" which is the source for the content
- Another useful feature could be a **quick search** function which would allow to filter files by search terms

----

**Pros**

- Better overview
- Information about file available where I need it
- Can avoid the "*I just start this and this and this and this notebook and scroll down in all of them until I find what I was looking for*"-situation
- Slight taste of "project support"

**Cons:**

- Potential performance impact (?)

issue_title: Consider pinning your spaCy version in requirements.txt?
issue_body:
I just noticed that your requirements.txt doesn't pin to any particular version of spaCy or NLTK. 

We've recently pushed spaCy 2, and while we've endeavoured to keep breaking changes to a minimum, it's a pretty big release: https://github.com/explosion/spaCy/releases/tag/v2.0.2

Even if the API doesn't change, there's the potential for problematic train/test skew for you if we make bug fixes to the tokenization, especially for languages other than English. Our compatibility policy is that changes that can affect statistical models can be made on minor releases --- e.g. spaCy 2.1.0 might fix some bug in the Hungarian tokenizer that affects a large number of tokens for that language. This means that sometimes, models trained with one minor version will suffer decreased accuracy if another version of the library is used at runtime. 

There are also potential performance considerations. There's currently an open ticket about performance degradation of the tokenizer. It's unfortunate that this problem made it into the release, and we're working on it. But in the meantime, users who make a new installation of `torch.text` might find their preprocessing is much slower.

issue_title: make flexible tooltips for LZ
issue_body:
### different sets of attributes for each phenotype

LocusZoom requires me to specify which attributes will exist (`layout.panels[#id=association].data_layers[#id=associationpvalues].fields`).  But I want users to be able to use whichever attributes they want.  For now, I'll have a constant set of attributes for each pheweb, but later I'll likely have a different set for each phenotype _within_ a pheweb.

I'd like to skip straight to dynamically setting `fields` for each phenotype.

Options:

1. Know which attributes we have before we serve our javascript, and modify it server-side.

2. On the client, modify things after receiving data.  `.getData(...)` is probably the best place to do that.  Chris didn't like that idea, and I didn't understand why not.  Look into `parseResponse`, especially the one in `GeneSource`.

### tooltips

LocusZoom tooltip templating only allows substituting values, and nothing more complicated.  Different phewebs have different attributes to show.  Soon even different phenotypes within the same pheweb will have different attributes. Later, within a single phenotype, different variants will have different attributes.  (eg, GWAS Catalog hits).  On the LZ-PheWAS, different points have different attributes.

I'd love to just have a function that takes a variant and returns HTML.  What if the tooltip just included an id and `<script>foo(variant)</script>`?

### Conversation with Chris:
-    // just follow the docs at https://github.com/statgen/locuszoom
-    // plot.on('data_rendered', () => { modify this.**.tooltip })
-    // but .fields is gonna be a problem
-    // I could hackishly modify it in the .getData
-    // or I can make the parseResponse &c act more forgiving like in GeneSource.

issue_title: Make toxenv match run environments
issue_body:
Currently the `toxenv` after all option looks at the TOXENV environment variable. This is mostly useful, because it's quite common to set that environment variable to control tox, and tox-travis uses it under the hood.

However, it fails in a couple of cases:

1. No TOXENV is set. This is mostly a non-concern for us, because we always set it.
2. `-e env` is given to `tox`. This is the more annoying part, because it _might_ be used. It's a reasonable limitation for a first version, but there's probably going to be someone somewhere that is surprised by this, and I won't blame them.

What I'd like to do, if possible, is inspect Tox's run somehow, so that I know which envs were actually run, and match against those instead of relying on the environment variable.

issue_title: Unclosed response/connection errors
issue_body:
Here's a simplified version of the code I have for fetching all objects from an S3 bucket:

``` python
import os
import asyncio
import aiobotocore

session = aiobotocore.get_session()
client = session.create_client('s3')

async def fetch(summary):
    response = await client.get_object(
        Bucket=os.environ['S3_BUCKET_NAME'],
        Key=summary['Key'],
    )

    return await response['Body'].read()

async def main():
    response = await client.list_objects_v2(
        Bucket=os.environ['S3_BUCKET_NAME'],
    )

    for summary in response.get('Contents', []):
        body = await fetch(summary)

        print(summary['Key'], len(body))

loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
loop.run_until_complete(main())
loop.close()

client.close()
```

file_B.json 38679

issue_title: Implement gas estimation
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

We need to implement gas estimation for the JSON-RPC interface.

An ideal gas estimation API should take into account the following.

- total gas used isn't always right due to gas refunds.
- transactions can alter execution flow based on the txn gas.

### How can it be fixed

This should be implemented using a swappable backend API.  Accurate gas estimations are likely to be computationally expensive where-as naive estimations based on gas used are likely to work for most use cases and should be computationally cheap.

We should first implement a `PeekGasUsedStrategy` which uses the peek gas usage

1. run the computation with a *high* gas limit (I think geth uses `50000000`) .
2. return the `computation.gas_meter.start_gas - computation.gas_metea.gas_remaining` for the outer computation.

Then we should implement what I believe is the current strategy used by go-ethereum which starts with running the transaction at `50000000` gas and then performs a binary search, re-running the transaction at each level of the search until it finds the threshold for which the transaction succeeds/fails.

issue_title: Auto-detect providers if none provided to Web3
issue_body:
* Version: 4

### What was wrong?

More and more functions are valuable even if not connected to a node. Initializing with no providers available would be helpful to access those functions.

Additionally, it would be natural to try to auto-detect providers if none are provided, equivalent to how `from web.auto import w3` works.

### How can it be fixed?

#### Specifying No Providers

To prevent connection to any node, allow initialization of `Web3` with `None` provider specified. This is already technically supported, it just fails with unhelpful error messages at the moment.

```
from web3 import Web3
w3 = Web3(providers=None)
# or simply
w3 = Web3(None)
```

If any call attempt is made 

#### Auto-detecting Providers

To auto-detect providers, allow initialization of `Web3` with no provider specified:

```
from web3 import Web3
w3 = Web3()
```

A `Web3` instance is returned immediately, and can be used for anything that does not interact with the node.

The first time a call to a node is attempted, it tries to auto-detect a node. If one is found, save it for later use and automatically reuse it. In this mode, any time the connection fails, it will try to reconnect to any node that it can find.

#### Auto-detection in `web3.auto.w3`

```
from web3.auto import w3
```

Will now be exactly equivalent to:

```
from web3 import Web3
w3 = Web3()
```

That means two changes:

1. If no nodes are found, the import still succeeds and works for functions that don't need node interaction
2. If a node is not found at import time, `w3` will attempt to re-detect it on every attempt to call to the node

#### Explicitly Setting Providers

*No change in functionality*

For the normal use case (previously, the only explicitly supported use case), `Web3` would only attempt to use the providers specified. If none of them are available, it will **not** attempt to auto-detect another one. (If you try to connect to a testnet node and fail, we don't want to automatically connect you to a production node without you realizing)

So sorry for filing an issue about this but I've looked literally everywhere in the documentation for both powerline and oh-my-zsh, where I installed it from, and  and come up with nothing. What does this orange '1' mean in my power line status bar?

issue_title: Strange bitrates reported, e.g. 320141 instead of 320000
issue_body:
When extracting the bitrate from many mp3 files, I get strange values such as 128111, 192167, 256222, or 320141 instead of the standards 128000, 192000, 256000, and 320000.

As I can only see the standard values in [mutagen/mp3/__init__.py](https://github.com/quodlibet/mutagen/blob/master/mutagen/mp3/__init__.py), I infer these numbers have to come from the file itself. Though the bitrate is correctly reported by other tools. Take for example [this audio file](http://freemusicarchive.org/music/Tours/Enthusiast/Tours_-_Enthusiast). With mutagen I get:
```
>>> f = mutagen.File('Tours_-_01_-_Enthusiast.mp3')
>>> f.info.bitrate, f.info.bitrate_mode
(320141, <BitrateMode.CBR: 1>)
```
With [eyeD3](https://eyed3.readthedocs.io):
```
>>> f = eyed3.load('Tours_-_01_-_Enthusiast.mp3')
>>> f.info.bit_rate
(False, 320)
```
Exiftool gives:
```
>>> exiftool Tours_-_01_-_Enthusiast.mp3
Audio Bitrate                   : 320 kbps
Encoder                         : LAME3.98r
Lame Method                     : CBR
Lame Bitrate                    : 255 kbps
```
Any idea? (What does "Lame Bitrate" mean BTW?)

issue_title: TypeError raised when casting InputPeerSelf to any kind of int in utils.get_peer_id
issue_body:
I am using the latest asyncio branch 06af73e  ：

```
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_client.py", line 2385, in get_entity
    for x in inputs
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_client.py", line 2385, in <listcomp>
    for x in inputs
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/utils.py", line 462, in get_peer_id
    _raise_cast_fail(peer, 'int')
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/utils.py", line 82, in _raise_cast_fail
    type(entity).__name__, target))
TypeError: Cannot cast InputPeerSelf to any kind of int.
```
[This line of code](https://github.com/LonamiWebs/Telethon/blob/06af73ed653062444d60205fb97151e7ed032e53/telethon/telegram_client.py#L2456) says `get_input_entity` will return InputPeerUser, InputPeerChat, or InputPeerChannel, but [actual code ](https://github.com/LonamiWebs/Telethon/blob/06af73ed653062444d60205fb97151e7ed032e53/telethon/telegram_client.py#L2459) return InputPeerSelf, so [this line](https://github.com/LonamiWebs/Telethon/blob/06af73ed653062444d60205fb97151e7ed032e53/telethon/telegram_client.py#L2384) will raise Type error when calling `utils.get_peer_id` with `InputPeerSelf`.

Should it be changed to `return await self.get_me(input_peer=True)`?

I'm trying to create an ApplicationOutput[0] with a list of Outputs and the creation fails with the message: `Property validation failure: [Value of property {/Output} does not match type {Object}]`.

`pydatajson` es uno de los proyectos de código abierto que como _librería funcional_ consideramos un producto prolijo y acabado.

El código en este repositorio tiene que estar bien documentado, bien testeado y seguir estándares de estilo.

**Propuesta**

Vamos a formalizar el abordaje de estos aspectos de a poco, de manera que incorporemos al build de Travis algunas de estas reglas.

**Primero:** incorporar un linter de PEP8 al build de Travis, para impedir la carga de código que no cumpla con este estándar de estilo.

issue_title: Can't assign a job's dataset if a namedtuple is instantiated in a script
issue_body:
I have a rather bizarre problem. Take this script:

```python
from collections import namedtuple

UserTuple = namedtuple('UserTuple', ['foo', 'bar'])
ut = UserTuple('baz', 123)

j = Job()
j.inputdata = []
```

Then run with Ganga 7.1.1 as a script:

```

```

One gets this traceback:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):

  File "/cvmfs/ganga.cern.ch/Ganga/install/7.1.1/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ganga/GangaCore/Runtime/bootstrap.py", line 1111, in run
    execfile(script, local_ns)
  File "tmp.py", line 7, in <module>
    j.inputdata = []

  File "/cvmfs/ganga.cern.ch/Ganga/install/7.1.1/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ganga/GangaCore/GPIDev/Base/Proxy.py", line 691, in __set__

  File "/cvmfs/ganga.cern.ch/Ganga/install/7.1.1/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ganga/GangaCore/GPIDev/Base/Proxy.py", line 657, in _process_set_value
    new_val = stripper(val)
  File "/cvmfs/ganga.cern.ch/Ganga/install/7.1.1/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ganga/GangaCore/GPIDev/Base/Proxy.py", line 647, in <lambda>
    stripper = lambda v: stripComponentObject(v, cfilter, item)

issue_title: Proxy round-tripping is broken
issue_body:
In latest develop
```python
from Ganga.GPIDev.Base.Proxy import addProxy, stripProxy
l = LocalFile()
assert l is addProxy(stripProxy(l))
```
does not pass but it used to, even as recent as 6.1.14. This was broken I think in #163 (specifically 55d7098cdac079) where I pointed it out but didn't realise the extent of the change. It was presented then as a performance change but really we want to be careful about object identity breaking.

I think that for correctness and compatibility with previous version of Ganga I would like to change this behaviour back by attaching a `_proxyObject` attribute to a `GangaObject` from Proxy.py if it's been wrapped. Of course we want to avoid `GangaObject` having any knowledge of this and so I also propose changing the "do not copy" logic in Objects.py to an "only copy these" logic.

This is not important enough to warrant a change in 6.1.16 but would make sense soon after.

issue_title: Possible incorrect entry in sys.path when using CPython 3.5.4
issue_body:
CPython 3.5.4 has a regression where the current directory may end up on sys.path when not using isolated mode and executing a zipfile or directory: https://bugs.python.org/issue32551

While I *haven't* confirmed definitively that the bug affects pex files, I also haven't been able to find anything in the pex code that asks the underlying interpreter to run in isolated mode, so my current assumption is that you'll be affected by the problem.

issue_title: Do not run hooks on unstaged files when commiting
issue_body:
In the documentation it says:
>pre-commit only runs on the staged contents of files by temporarily saving the contents of your files at commit time and stashing the unstaged changes while running hooks.

This is true when I use the command `pre-commit run` unstaged files do not get processed.

However it is not the case when I commit to repos (`git commit`): the unstaged files get processed, leading failed the tests when the staged files pass the tests.

This is because an completely different bash script is generated for hooks/pre-commit, instead of just executing `pre-commit run` with some parameters to match the configuration. (which is what I'm doing manually replacing it with)

Are there any reasons as to why it's done this way? To me it seems unintuitive to do it both ways, creating a difference of functionality

issue_title: maxResults behaviour is inconsistent with Jira API
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
`JIRA._fetch_pages` has the ability to iterate through paged results. This function uses `maxResults` to determine whether to iterate through all pages, or whether to return the first page only. This means that in order to fetch all pages of results instead of just the first page, `maxResults` must be set to `None`.

In Jira's API, `maxResults` only defines how many items to return *per page*, and has no relationship to how many pages of results are available or how many pages can be returned. It is not intended to control the total number of results available from an API call: total results are limited by `total` parameter. `maxResults` is primarily intended to manage the memory requirements of returning too many issues at once (e.g. with a low max heap you might reduce `maxResults` to 25, resulting in more pages but less memory usage per page).

Because `maxResults` has no relation to the number of pages available in Jira itself, its current use in determining whether to page through results is inconsistent with the Jira API and doesn't make much sense.

**To Reproduce**
This is most obvious when using `JIRA.search_issues`, where `maxResults=50` is the default value. Paging through issues must be performed manually, unless you set `maxResults=None` (in which case, Jira *still* uses its default `maxResults` value of 50, only now `JIRA.search_issues` will handle paging for you).

 
```
>>> jira = JIRA(JIRA_SERVER, basic_auth(JIRA_USER, JIRA_PASS))
>>> jql_str = """project=MYPROJ""" # some JQL that will return more than 50 issues
>>> ### Query for issues using default options
>>> len(jira.search_issues(jql))
50
>>> ### Query for issues setting no default value for max results per page
>>> len(jira.search_issues(jql, maxResults=None))
1000
```

**Expected behavior**
Setting `maxResults` should not affect automatic paging behaviour. There may be legitimate reasons to want to limit results to 25 per page (say a small max heap in the JVM), whilst retaining automatic paging.

**Suggested solution**
Putting aside the use of `maxResults` to define unrelated behaviour, if it is desired to only return one page of results then defining an explicit `iter_pages=True` option in the function signature that can be overridden via the module's public interfaces would serve that purpose.

Note: Correct or not I realise this would constitute a breaking change, so it would make sense to address this in the 2.0 release required to address Python 3.7 compatibility.

Also, full disclosure: I work for Atlassian (and I love your work here!) I've been using this library for a while now and have always hand written paging code. I was planning to open a PR to add the feature when I found out it already exists. Happy to send a PR to address this issue, and to provide an `iter_pages` option to use in the current logic rather than removing that logic altogether.

**Version Information**
Python Interpreter: 3.7
jira-python: jira-2.0.0.0rc1
OS: macOS

issue_title: Release 0.1.2
issue_body:
@andfoy, please release **0.1.2**. Important things to do:

1. Close this issue before creating our Changelog. An issue informing about a new release must be created every time so that the rest of the team knows about it.
2. Close the Github milestone before creating our Changelog so that it's created with the right date format.
3. Update our conda-forge feedstock.
4. Move conda-forge packages to the spyder-ide channel once they are uploaded to anaconda.org. Our installation instructions must not include conda-forge because installing from conda-forge is one of the main causes of breakage in Anaconda (when not done in a different environment from root, which almost nobody does).

issue_title: Plugin request: USTVnow
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [ ] This is a bug report.
- [ ] This is a feature request.
- [x] This is a plugin (improvement) request.
- [x] I have read the contribution guidelines.

### Description

Hi, i would like to request a plugin for [USTVnow](https://www.ustvnow.com/). They offer [free](https://www.ustvnow.com/register.php?sub=7) access to [7 US TV Channels](https://support.ustvnow.com/hc/en-us/articles/214145143-What-plans-channels-are-available-) and paid access to 30 channels, [some in HD](https://support.ustvnow.com/hc/en-us/articles/214145203-What-USTVnow-channels-are-available-in-HD-on-the-paid-plans-).

Access-URL:
http://m.ustvnow.com/iphone (iStuff)
http://www.ustvnow.com/gtv/ (SmartTVs)
http://watch.ustvnow.com/guide (Webbrowsers)

The direct links are javascript-obfuscated and i can't see deep-links to the programs

They use various protocols, including HLS and RTMP, **but HLS access only works for 30 days on the free account**. HLS seems to offer a bit more quality (even on the SD-Only free account)

There are already several projects in python that can produce an m3u playlist (the urls are only valid for about 30mins):

issue_title: Tensorboard not showing whole results
issue_body:
I've trained a DQN for a Reinforcement Learning project. For this I've used a remote server. After 1 night of iterations I've copied the folder locally and explored the summaries in tensorboard.

I've noticed that in this static conditions (the summary files don't get updated anymore) tensorboard keeps updating my result as usual every 30 seconds.

To make it clear I'll do an example. I've started iterating at 17.30 of Day 1 until 8.00 of Day 2. My tensorboard right now after 15 minutes, shows results from 17.30 til 21.00.

There is actually a way to show the complete result? Or do I need to wait in order to allow tensorboard reading the file completely?

I had a look on the documentation and it seems like there is not a solution. But I am still confused.

From Tensorboard Github Official:

_TensorBoard is showing only some of my data, or isn't properly updating!
This issue usually comes about because of how TensorBoard iterates through the tfevents files: it progresses through the events file in timestamp order, and only reads one file at a time. Let's suppose we have files with timestamps a and b, where a<b. Once TensorBoard has read all the events in a, it will never return to it, because it assumes any new events are being written in the more recent file. This could cause an issue if, for example, you have two FileWriters simultaneously writing to the same directory. If you have multiple summary writers, each one should be writing to a separate directory._

Could anyone clarify this issue? I am new with Tensorflow.

Thank you in advance

IE11 is having the same issue ? Or it has been fixed in IE 11 ?

issue_title: Test Cases must provide the approvee field
issue_body:
Polarion now requires that `Test Cases` should provide the `approvee` field. I think that we can use the user running the script to populate this field at runtime.

**IMPORTANT**: the user running the script **must** have the `project_approver` role in order to be able to approve a `Test Case`.

Here's an example:

```python
testcase = TestCase(project_id='RHSAT6', work_item_id='RHSAT6-2726')
testcase.add_approvee('omaciel')
testcase.update()
testcase.edit_approval('omaciel', 'approved')
```

issue_title: hbmqtt accepts UTF8 topic strings with illegal characters in PUBLISH
issue_body:
> [MQTT-1.5.3-1]
> The character data in a UTF-8 encoded string MUST be well-formed UTF-8 as defined by the Unicode specification [Unicode] and restated in RFC 3629 [RFC3629]. In particular this data MUST NOT include encodings of code points between U+D800 and U+DFFF. If a Server or Client receives a Control Packet containing ill-formed UTF-8 it MUST close the Network Connection.
 
> [MQTT-1.5.3-2]
> A UTF-8 encoded string MUST NOT include an encoding of the null character U+0000. If a receiver (Server or Client) receives a Control Packet containing U+0000 it MUST close the Network Connection.

> [MQTT-1.5.3-3]
> A UTF-8 encoded sequence 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF is always to be interpreted to mean U+FEFF ("ZERO WIDTH NO-BREAK SPACE") wherever it appears in a string and MUST NOT be skipped over or stripped off by a packet receiver.

issue_title: Sandbox/Test mode for certonly operation
issue_body: When setting up the automation to renew certificates (in case you are using the "manual" modes like `certonly` or with CSRs). Some steps might need to be repeated several times until everything works as expected. It would be nice if the client supported such a testing mode, by e.g. issuing only self-signed certificates, so that you do not actually request real certificates and easily end up in the rate limit and waste resources of the CA.

issue_title: Apache --redirect doesn't work in the presence of RewriteRules
issue_body: It was probably written that way in order to be cautious about overlapping regexp semantics.  But it should be safe to add a HTTP -> HTTPS redirect after other redirects have fired, even if that winds up being redundant.

issue_title: Functions in reverter.py should defer KeyboardInterrupt and similar kill signals
issue_body: Many of the operations in `reverter.py` should be treated as atomic, to the greatest extent possible.  They should begin by registering a handler for these events, and end by raising an exception if any such events arrived.

issue_title: DataCollector carrying over information
issue_body:
We recently did an adaptation of the Wolf Sheep example to try and build a disease model (susceptible, infected, recovered) in mesa. When we plotted the population sizes, as is done in the wolf sheep model, the chart plots correctly the first time. However, when we reset the parameters and try and run a new model, the chart that shows up in the server **first** displays the data from the previous model's execution, and **then** it shows the data from the next iteration. 

An example is shown below:

![screen_shot 2018-04-17 at 4 27 27 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25071352/38894956-b6718970-425c-11e8-8ce5-052b22182265.jpg)

The sharp discontinuity in the graph is where the new data for the model starts being displayed. We then thought that it may have been our model (a bug), but revisiting the wold sheep model we saw the same thing

![screen_shot 2018-04-17 at 4 27 34 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25071352/38895039-f41f8efc-425c-11e8-894d-18b155dd6c88.jpg)

Is there a known issue where the DataCollector does not reset between runs? Closing the kernel and restarting it fixes the issue.

Our code can be found [here](https://github.com/nathawkins/CMSE202--Semester-Project/tree/master/agent_based_virus).

issue_title: Improve SentinelAPI.query interface
issue_body:
When using `SentinelAPI` from a scripts, I found it a bit tricky to figure out what to provide to the `SentinelAPI.query` function:

1. `area` - this needs to be a string of coordinates formatted in a special way. We have a function for reading a geojson file ([`get_coordinates`](https://github.com/ibamacsr/sentinelsat/blob/v0.9.1/sentinelsat/sentinel.py#L425)). But it includes both, **reading** the geojson file and **formatting** the string. I suggest to separate the two steps, so users can use the formatting function on its own with a coordinates sequence e.g. from `fiona` or some other source of geojson-like information.
2. `initial_date` and `end_date` - we parse these with [`_format_date`](https://github.com/ibamacsr/sentinelsat/blob/v0.9.1/sentinelsat/sentinel.py#L472) but that function **does not fail** when receiving some invalid input but just passes it on. I think fail it should.

The error the user gets from mistakes in the formats is some very very cryptic `SentinelAPIError` with lots Java traceback information from COAHub, because the query payload *is* generated but invalid. Very hard to figure out what went wrong.

Regarding the `area` parameter, we could also consider to do the string formatting **inside** the `query` function. Then the user just has to provide a (well-defined) geojson `coordinates` sequence. Would you support this, @willemarcel, @Fernerkundung and @valgur?

issue_title: Allow (numeric-)ranges for ICMP-type/code
issue_body:
<!---
Verify first that your issue/request is not already reported on GitHub.
Also test if the latest release, and master branch are affected too.
-->

##### ISSUE TYPE
<!--- Pick one below and delete the rest: -->
 - Feature Idea

##### SUMMARY
<!--- Explain the problem briefly -->

Only for 4.xx! (because configuration parsing seems to be more complex in 3.x, and more changes are required to achive the same):

As discussed previously flow-spec encoding for icmp-type/code is pretty much the same as IP-protocol. This way of encoding allows to go for ranges of icmp-type/code (like already implemented for IP-protocol in exabgp 4.x - no reason to restrict icmp-type/code to only a selection of _named_ codes/types).

Configurations like this should be perfectly acceptable:

```
icmp-type [ >=1&<=4 >=10&<=20 ] ;
or
icmp-type [ >echo-reply&<echo-request >=10&<=20 ] ;
or 
icmp-type [ >echo-reply&<5 >=10&<=20 ] ;
```

For me this seems to do what I currently need (in 4.x):

```
--- a/lib/exabgp/bgp/message/update/nlri/flow.py
+++ b/lib/exabgp/bgp/message/update/nlri/flow.py
@@ -403,14 +403,14 @@ class FlowSourcePort (IOperationByteShort,NumericString,IPv4,IPv6):
 class FlowICMPType (IOperationByte,NumericString,IPv4,IPv6):
        ID = 0x07
        NAME = 'icmp-type'
-       converter = staticmethod(converter(ICMPType.named))
+       converter = staticmethod(converter(ICMPType.named,ICMPType))
        decoder = staticmethod(decoder(_number,ICMPType))
 
 
 class FlowICMPCode (IOperationByte,NumericString,IPv4,IPv6):
        ID = 0x08
        NAME = 'icmp-code'
-       converter = staticmethod(converter(ICMPCode.named))
+       converter = staticmethod(converter(ICMPCode.named,ICMPCode))
        decoder = staticmethod(decoder(_number,ICMPCode))
``` 

##### IMPORTANCE
<!-- Please let us know if the issue is affecting you in a production environment -->
low - testing

issue_title: AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'value', Bug?
issue_body:
I am running `AvroConsumer` and it throws the following error:

```
avro-test_1  |   File "/usr/local/bin/main.py", line 39, in <module>
avro-test_1  |     msg = consumer.poll(timeout=1.0)
avro-test_1  |   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/confluent_kafka/avro/__init__.py", line 151, in poll
avro-test_1  |     if not message.value() and not message.key():
avro-test_1  | AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'value'
```

`/confluent_kafka/avro/__init__.py` says:

```
        message = super(AvroConsumer, self).poll(timeout)
        if not message.value() and not message.key():
            return message
        if not message.error():
            if message.value() is not None:
```

So it looks like it tries to access `.value` even before checking if the message existed.
Is it a bug?

issue_title: Handling Metrics in Basescript.
issue_body:
@Basha-Shaik Notes from @prashanthellina 

**Requirement:**
- We need to collect metrics in `Basescript`.
- We already have a code but it is complicated and tied to `InfluxDB` format.
- We decided it is not the right design architecture to do at `Basescript` code.
- And it is also complicated because it is trying to merge multiple metrics and write consolidated entries.
- It is confusing for people to figure out when to write a `log` and `metric` and the difference between them.
- Also, we are not able to bind `tags` to the `metrics` object but we can do that for the log object
**Refer:** https://hackmd.io/MYMw7CAMAmwIwFoAscQA5kEM4FYFoE4A2IhEIgpAZhByOiJwKA==?both

**Sample Proposed Metric Design:**
```
self.log.info('measurement_name', type="metric", tag1="blah", tag2="foo", value1=0.5, value2=2)
```

issue_title: Refactor testing of scikit-build sample projects
issue_body:
Currently [ctest is invoked](https://github.com/scikit-build/scikit-build/blob/26dae334d705805d95ac6d99571e0284fd818a09/tests/test_pen2.py#L39-L42) to run test of each sample projects and we should also [avoid explicitly setting](https://github.com/scikit-build/scikit-build/blob/26dae334d705805d95ac6d99571e0284fd818a09/tests/samples/pen2-cython/scripts/CMakeLists.txt#L14) `PYTHONPATH`. Mocking with `PYTHONPATH` is causing problem with tools like a `tox`.

Instead, sample project level tests:
* should be implemented as regular python test using either `node` or python `unittest`.
* sample project tests should be executed in two contexts:
 * developer mode / editable package mode
 * installed package mode

issue_title: Subclassed ndarray attributes are dropped when stored as attributes in another class
issue_body:
Hi,

I was trying to use dill to serialize some custom classes I have implemented in python that store some custom subclassed ndarrays.

for example

```
import numpy as np

import dill

class RealisticInfoArray(np.ndarray):

    def __new__(cls, input_array, info=None):
        # Input array is an already formed ndarray instance
        # We first cast to be our class type
        obj = np.asarray(input_array).view(cls)
        # add the new attribute to the created instance
        obj.info = info
        # Finally, we must return the newly created object:
        return obj

    def __array_finalize__(self, obj):
        # see InfoArray.__array_finalize__ for comments
        if obj is None: return
        self.info = getattr(obj, 'info', None)


class MyContainer:

    def __init__(self, things):

        self.things = things


my_things = RealisticInfoArray([1, 2, 3], info='a')

my_box = MyContainer(my_things)

new_box = dill.loads(dill.dumps(my_box))

print(my_box.things.info)
print(new_box.things.info)

```

running this code results in

a

Is there some way to make dill include this information in the serialization?

issue_title: Only request necessary fields when Querying Esri Services
issue_body:
An idea, not sure where to implement.

For massive ArcGIS Server-backed services, there may be a significant reduction in response (and processing) time if the request only returned the fields needed for the conform object. Take NY State for example, 4M+ addresses, runs out of time caching.

Here's a request with only the necessary fields + geometry :
http://gisservices.dhses.ny.gov/arcgis/rest/services/SAM_Address_Points/MapServer/1/query?where=1%3D1&text=&objectIds=&time=&geometry=&geometryType=esriGeometryEnvelope&inSR=&spatialRel=esriSpatialRelIntersects&relationParam=&outFields=CompleteStreetName%2C+AddressNumber&returnGeometry=true&maxAllowableOffset=&geometryPrecision=&outSR=&returnIdsOnly=false&returnCountOnly=false&orderByFields=&groupByFieldsForStatistics=&outStatistics=&returnZ=false&returnM=false&gdbVersion=&returnDistinctValues=false&returnTrueCurves=false&resultOffset=&resultRecordCount=&f=pjson

178ms response, 20862 bytes

Here's one with outfields=* (how machine currently runs):
http://gisservices.dhses.ny.gov/arcgis/rest/services/SAM_Address_Points/MapServer/1/query?where=1%3D1&text=&objectIds=&time=&geometry=&geometryType=esriGeometryEnvelope&inSR=&spatialRel=esriSpatialRelIntersects&relationParam=&outFields=*&returnGeometry=true&maxAllowableOffset=&geometryPrecision=&outSR=&returnIdsOnly=false&returnCountOnly=false&orderByFields=&groupByFieldsForStatistics=&outStatistics=&returnZ=false&returnM=false&gdbVersion=&returnDistinctValues=false&returnTrueCurves=false&resultOffset=&resultRecordCount=&f=pjson

299 ms, 75041 byes (almost twice as long and 4x as large).

If the conform object can be loaded before caching to geojson begins, these requests could be made substantially smaller (and the resulting temp files on disk would be smaller too).

issue_title: Recipe for Snapshot'testing
issue_body:
I'm going to leave this here, and it can be closed. It's not really related to `graphene`, either, but because `snapshottest` is documented alongside `graphene` I figured this would be a handy place to share a recipe capturing type definitions.

```python
from graphql.utils.introspection_query import introspection_query

def get_type_definitions(schema, *type_names, **kwargs):
    result = schema.execute(introspection_query, **kwargs)
    assert not result.errors
    typemap = {
        type_['name']: type_
        for type_ in result.data['__schema']['types']
    }
    return {type_name: typemap[type_name] for type_name in type_names}

def test_my_mutation_type(schema, session):
    definitions = get_type_definitions(
        schema,
        str(MyMutationType),
        str(MyMutationType.Input),
    )
    snapshot.assert_match(definitions)
```

While I'm not a fan of the fact that `graphene.ObjectType` overrides `type.__str__` in the broken-way that it does*, a user can also supply `MyMutationType._meta.name` (how I actually prefer to do it).

* [bonus] the broken `__str__` implementation:
```python
import graphene

class X(graphene.ObjectType):
    class Meta:
        abstract = True

print(X)  # AttributeError: type object 'X' has no attribute '_meta'
```

Again, this is closable, just wanted to pass along the recipe.

adding count to query
Updates to RxPY (vNext)
issue_body:
As a heads up, I was talking with the maintainer of RxPY and there will be major changes to the library by year's end. This major change will be both a refactor of RxPY and will also support the notion of an `AsyncObservable`, bridged from the `aioreactive` library: [RxPy vNext](https://github.com/ReactiveX/RxPY/tree/vnext).

I think the major benefit to `graphql-core` will be that we could remove the [`AsyncgenObservable`](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core/blob/master/graphql/execution/executors/asyncio_utils.py#L55) as this should be natively supported (from [`aioreactive`](https://github.com/dbrattli/aioreactive#asynchronous-iteration)).

issue_title: support nested objects
Resource name defined in view is not used in related relationships
issue_body:
Hello! I just want to describe something that has led me to confusion today.

The problem is this statement:

`
If you set the resource_name on a combination of model, serializer, or view in the same hierarchy, the name will be resolved as following: view > serializer > model. (Ex: A view resource_name will always override a resource_name specified on a serializer or model)
`

Say you have the following:

```
class PassengerSerializer(serializer.ModelSerializer):
   class Meta:
       resource_name = 'passenger-type-serializer'
       model = Passenger

class CarSerializer(serializer.ModelSerializer):
   passengers = ResourceRelatedField(many=True, source='passengers', read_only=True)

   include_serializers = dict(passengers=PassengerSerializer)
   class Meta:
       model = Car

class Passenger(viewsets.ReadOnlyModelViewSet):
   resource_name = 'passenger-type-view'
   serializer_class = PassengerSerializer

class Car(viewsets.ReadOnlyModelViewSet):
   serializer_class = CarSerializer

```
And you have a url configuration such that these resources are available through `/cars/` and `/passengers/`

Doing:
```GET /passengers/1/``` will return `type=passenger-type-view`

while:

```GET /cars/1/?include=passengers``` will return an `included` key with a list of resources with `type=passenger-type-serializer`

This is a bit inconsistent as depending from where you 'read' the passenger resource you might get different values for the `type` key.

To fix this, the `ResourceRelatedField` should be aware of the view the resource is exposed through, so that the `resource_name` value from the view could be read. This is not possible.

What do you think? Maybe it's not the best idea to allow people to define resource names in the views and that should just stay in the serializers/models.

Cheers!

issue_title: RFC JSON Schema-based record editor (replacement for BibEdit)
issue_body:
Problem
-----------
`BibEdit` served well in Invenio `1.x` but is MARC-centric and is not designed for extensibility. Since Invenio is moving towards using *jsonalchemy* as its record representation (thus opening the ports for infinitely flexible record models), an hardcoded table-based web editor is no longer sufficient to natively edit Invenio records.

Proposal
-----------
Since *jsonalchemy* is about (inveniosoftware/jsonalchemy#9) to support [JSON Schemas](http://json-schema.org/) which is a proposed standard to describe JSON models (including validation), we could automatically generate a form that allows the cataloger to edit a given record according to the schema. This would allow the system to leverage on data models, for more and more consistent records.
It happens that this is already implemented thanks to [json-editor](https://github.com/jdorn/json-editor). E.g. see: http://jeremydorn.com/json-editor/. 

Starting from this pre-existing editor one can build around/on top of it additional functionalities such as:
- [ ] record preview
- [ ] PDF preview
- [ ] auto-completion based on KBs
- [ ] automatic linking (e.g. when editing an arXiv ID, to be able to jump to it)
- [ ] server side validation based on JSONAlchemy
- [ ] keyboard shortcuts
- [ ] re-ordering through drag&drop
- [ ] editing references to other records (e.g. author signatures, affiliations, citations, superseeding... see #2573)
- [ ] shurtcuts such as *automatically generating new field instances by splitting the content of a given field
- [ ] isbn check for duplicates
- [ ] Implement shell like short cuts to jump directly to a given field (e.g. Ctrl-R)

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/119349/6514194/cc4c9bbc-c380-11e4-88f1-262b6e85d168.png)

issue_title: Also use CFG_OAI_ID_FIELD for deduping
issue_body:
It seems advisable that `find_records_from_extoaiid()` also considers `CFG_OAI_ID_FIELD` for record matching.

Usecase: 
1. join2 repositories harvest each other to get copies from records. We use this process e.g. for authority records.
2. join2 repositories act as institutional bibliographies, this implies to collect all publications from a given institution (even if it's also catalogued at another partner)

In case of _authorities_ the authority ID in 035 ensures deduping. Recent upcoming use cases, however, also involve harvesting of _bibliographic_ records, e.g. in case two institutions from the join2 collaboration work together, we want to ensure, that our scientists have to submit their publications only once. E.g. a scientist from Jülich should only need to submit to JuSER even if he would have to submit to iMPULSE as well. Harvesting ensures that publications from JuSER are exposed to iMPULSE. It doesn't make to much sense to move the OAI-ID to another field in this case. as we're really producing a second instance of the record in another service. At the same time from a bibliographic point of view usage of 035__a for oai-ids will not work in our environment as we need a field for _the id_(tm) (ie. the one that get's stored with the linked records) in case of authority records. This is currently done via 035__.

An alternative, more complex approach might be to allow a list of marc tags for `CFG_BIBUPLOAD_EXTERNAL_OAIID_TAG` probably consolidating with `CFG_BIBUPLOAD_EXTERNAL_SYSNO_TAG`.

join2 might come up with an implementation of the simple case. (`CFG_OAI_ID_FIELD`).

issue_title: templates: document ``current_i18n``
tests: simplify doctest execution
issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_title: get_window_data returns invalid json (with suggested fix)
issue_body:
https://github.com/facebookresearch/visdom/blob/8a3a6a4a8928693a240cf16efa6106f2a01a5827/py/visdom/server.py#L907-L917

I think 914-917 need to be removed. Looks like they were copied from `win_exists()`, but maybe left on accident. 

If the selected `win` does exist then the result is JSON + "true" appended on the end which makes the json invalid (json.loads() fails):

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20519655/39486683-a0d4d126-4d42-11e8-852b-564bd714bc89.png)

If the selected `win` does not exist then the assert fails so "false" never gets written. The returned result is an empty string with an exception logged on the server. 

I removed the lines locally and `json.loads(vis.get_window_data('compare'))` works like a champ and passing in a `win` that does not exist prints exception on server logs and returns an empty string. This seems like fine behavior if the `win` doesn't exists, but I'd leave that decision you guys. 

Related to PR: #272

issue_title: Problems with make_object; plan for deprecation/removal
issue_body:
`make_object` causes problems when applying `pre_load` decorators that change the shape of the data (e.g. adding an "envelope"). 

### Code to reproduce

```python
from marshmallow import Schema, fields, post_load

class MySchema(Schema):
    foo = fields.Field()

    @post_load(raw=True)
    def post_load(self, data, many):
        envelope = 'data' if many else 'datum'
        ret = {envelope: data}
        return ret

schema = MySchema(many=True)
result = schema.load([{'foo': 42}, {'foo': 24}])

print(result.data)
```

### Expected

```python
{'data': [{'foo': 42}, {'foo': 24}]}
```

### Actual

```python
['data']
```

The problem is in these lines: https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/cdc74fd78db011688e0f04017d87041e8e3d695a/marshmallow/schema.py#L606-L607. They assume that `result` is a list, but it might not be if a `pre_load` or `post_load` handler changed the data.

Some less severe consequences of `make_object` are that it (1) may lead to unnecessary iteration over deserialized results and (2) is redundant with `post_load`.

Hi

We are using marshmallow-jsonapi with SQLAlchemy and we have few cases where we need to retrieve a groups of objects involving some deep includes. One such example is 

GET /v3/portfolios/672/investments?fields[accounts]=name&fields[files]=url&fields[indicators]=name&fields[investment_invitations]=organization&fields[reports]=name,stage,status&fields[results]=forecast_current&filter[allocations.results.indicator.relevant]=true&include=allocations.report,allocations.report.results,allocations.report.results.indicator,allocations.report.logo_file,invitation,grantee

I looked into `marshmallow-jsonapi` and understand how include data is gathered and how that can generate lot's of queries.

Any suggestion will be highly welcome.

issue_title: Add if a variant has sanger ordered in the case report
Don't double-allocate LookupTable2D
issue_body:
Currently, when creating a `LookupTable2D`, the values array is stored as an attribute of the class (`.f`), and also gets sent down to c++ where it's stored again.  This isn't a huge problem for 1D arrays - where it also occurs, but are usually small - but adds up quickly for 2D arrays.  It's not hard to consume all 16 GB of my laptop's memory doing an atmospheric PSF simulation, for instance, and it would be great to recover ~50% of that.

We already have a `.getVals()` method for `_galsim._LookupTable2D` that returns a read-only view into the c++ layer values array.  I propose hooking `LookupTable2d.f` up to that as a property instead.  This saves RAM, and has the added benefit of making LookupTable2D more immutable in practice, not just principle.  I suppose hooking up `.x` and `.y` the same way would also be reasonable, as would be doing similar things to 1D `LookupTable`.  Thoughts?

issue_title: Adding default CSRF header as a good security practice .
issue_body:
It is no harm to set X-Requested-By when csrf protection is disabled. This will help user experience so when livy CsrfFilter check for "X-Requested-By" header it doesn't return a ""Missing Required Header for CSRF protection."
Check the usage of CSRF headers at owasp.
The main idea is to check the presence of a custom header (agreed-upon between the server and a client – e.g. X-CSRF or X-Requested-By) in all state-changing requests coming from the client.

def run():
    client = pubsub_v1.SubscriberClient()
    sub_name = 'projects/{}/subscriptions/{}'.format(PROJECT_ID, SUBSCRIPTION_NAME)
    subscription = client.subscribe(
        subscription=sub_name,
        callback=callback
    )
    while True:
        time.sleep(5)

def callback(message):
    success = process_message(message)
    if success:
        message.ack()
    # else:
    #     message.nack()

def process_message(message):
    print(message)
    return False

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run()
```

When I run this code, my client pulls messages on the queue once. It then does not ack the message, and the message returns to the queue after 10 seconds as expected. After a message returns to the queue, the client does however not pull the message again. If I explicitly nack the message, it does pull the message from the queue again.

I would expect the message to appear again in my client when I don’t nack, and the 10 seconds (default limit until it should appear again) has gone by.

issue_title: Client library should not try to validate sample rate
issue_body:
The client library should allow validation of sample rate to occur on the server rather than trying to do it's own validation.  Allowing the server to check errors means that we don't need to update the client if the server expands the range of sample rates that it supports.

https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/speech/google/cloud/speech/sample.py#L70

issue_title: folium crashes Jupyter Notebook when using large geojson file
issue_body:
**Note:** This issue is mostly copy/pasted from the below link:

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47660163/folium-crashes-jupyter-notebook-when-using-large-geojson-file

------

Versions (according to my conda environment YAML):

python=3.6
folium=0.5.0
branca=0.2.0
numpy=1.13.3
pandas=0.21.0
geopandas=0.3.0

(Although I don't think I even use all of the above packages)

------

I am trying to follow the example of adding a geo_json overlay found [here][1]. The geo_json file I am using is this map of the zip codes in Germany, found [here][2]. It is large at 85.8 mb.

Here is my MWE; I downloaded the 'postleitzahlen.geojson' file to (pwd being my "present working directory") 'pwd/data/postleitzahlen.geojson'. Note that the same problem happens even when I change the extension from the non-standard .geojson to .json.

    import folium
    # Not sure if I need all of the packages below:
    import json
    import os
    
    # Center map in middle of Berlin, zoom out enough so all of Germany is visible,
    # and use mapbox bright instead of default OpenStreetMap so as to hopefully make
    # images easier to render. But it still crashes the notebook anyway.
    m=folium.Map(location=[52.5194, 13.4067], tiles='Mapbox Bright', zoom_start=5)
    
    # This is the analog of the example on the Folium website, but I don't really understand it.
    # Wouldn't we need to load the file into memory somehow, maybe using geopandas or something?
    zipcode_regions = os.path.join('data', 'postleitzahlen.geojson')
    
    # Add the geoJSON layer to map ostensibly:
    folium.GeoJson(zipcode_regions, name='geo_json').add_to(m)
    
    # Still crashes regardless of whether I include following line - I think it just adds control in top-right of map on example website, which I don't need.
    folium.LayerControl().add_to(m)

    m

In Jupyter Notebook (on my computer at least) what happens is that for the output of the cell containing the last line, one just gets a large empty white space. Moreover, at the top it says 'Autosave Failed!'. And when one tries to click the save button, the notebook freezes momentarily, and then nothing happens. (I.e. there's no indication that the file was saved.)

I can still run new cells (e.g. performing basic arithmetic), but nothing saves. (EDIT: Nope, doing this too often causes the notebook to crash outright in Chrome.)

This might be a bug in either Jupyter, IPython, or Folium, in which case asking here might not help much. But I figured that I would try asking here at least. 

Looking at [the documentation for this function][3] (scroll down a lot), should I try either (1) setting the `overlay` parameter to `True`, since the default is `False`? (2) setting the `smooth_factor` to a float greater than the default of `1.0`? (I will try both and update this post with any results.)

I read these questions, but did not understand how to use their answers to solve my problem. If someone can explain how to apply those answers here, I would greatly appreciate it. [(1)][4][(2)][5][(3)][6][(4)][7][(5)][8]

**EDIT:** I tried doing [what this person did][9], namely increasing the data limit (specifically I ran `jupyter notebook --NotebookApp.iopub_data_rate_limit=1.0e10` to launch Jupyter Notebook) but the same error occurred as before.   
**EDIT (2):** Still occurs setting `overlay=True` and both using and not using data limit increase.  
**EDIT (3):** Setting `smooth_factor` to `10`, `100`, or `1000` didn't fix it, although it did make the penultimate cell run faster. So this seems more likely a problem with Jupyter than folium.   
The terminal output each time for Jupyter Notebook contains multiple errors of the form:  

    Saving file at /map.ipynb
    [I 12:00:00.000 NotebookApp] Malformed HTTP message from ::1: Content-Length too long

Watching the terminal more closely as the notebook runs, it is clear that this error occurs exactly as the notebook tries to load the map. So next I will try the solutions proposed [here][10].

**EDIT (4):** Still doesn't work trying the `jupyter notebook --NotebookApp.tornado_settings="{'max_body_size': 104857600, 'max_buffer_size': 104857600}"` suggested [here][10]; also doesn't work when adding three additional zeroes to `max_body_size` and `max_buffer_size`, nor when adding six additional zeroes to both. (I.e. [approx. 0.5 petabytes][11], a million times the defaults.)

Since the GeoJSON loads on GitHub (albeit slowly), it seems that it is possible for the GeoJSON to be loaded with a map. And 0.5 petabytes is probably not a reasonable limit for HTTP requests generated by folium to exceed, so hence why I am posting this as an issue here, although it might (I don't know) be an issue with Jupyter Notebook instead. It could also conceivably be an issue with leaflet too.

**EDIT:** Full code (but not minimal):

    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import folium
    import branca
    import geopandas as gpd

    import json
    import shelve
    import os

    m = folium.Map(location=[52.5194, 13.4067], tiles='Mapbox Bright', zoom_start=5)
    # Test that folium works without any geoJSON layer -- it does, and very quickly too.
    m
    zipcode_regions = os.path.join('data', 'postleitzahlen.json')
    folium.GeoJson(zipcode_regions, name='geo_json', overlay=True, smooth_factor=1000).add_to(m)
    # The cell above this isn't immediate, but fairly quick. When trying to run the following cell, the notebook crashes:
    m

  [2]: https://github.com/yetzt/postleitzahlen/blob/master/data/postleitzahlen.geojson
  [3]: http://python-visualization.github.io/folium/modules.html
  [4]: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/42107268/geojson-data-not-displaying-in-python-folium-map
  [5]: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/44297213/downloading-geojson-file-into-folium-in-jupyter
  [6]: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/26655531/python-folium-geojson-map-not-displaying
  [7]: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/37003421/folium-choropleth-geojson-raises-attributeerror-nonetype
  [8]: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45558954/geojson-in-ipython-display-cannot-be-imported-in-jupyter-notebook
  [9]: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/46431442/folium-maps-not-showing-up-on-jupyter-notebook-uploaded-on-github
  [10]: https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/650
  [11]: https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/notebook/notebookapp.py#L237-L238
